So in the main function, when we find a mismatch at left and right, we check two possibilities: check if the substring from left to right-1 is a palindrome (i.e., skip the right character) or if the substring from left+1 to right is a palindrome (skip the left character). public boolean validPalindrome(String s) {
    int left = 0;
    int right = s.length() - 1;
    while (left < right) {
        if (s.charAt(left) == s.charAt(right)) {
            left++;
            right--;
        } else {
            return isPalindrome(s, left+1, right) || isPalindrome(s, left, right-1);
        }
    }
    return true;
}

private boolean isPalindrome(String s, int i, int j) {
    while (i < j) {
        if (s.charAt(i++) != s.charAt(j--)) {
            return false;
        }
    }
    return true;
}

Now, considering the code's time complexity. The main loop is O(n), and in the worst case, the helper function runs O(n) time. But each character is checked once, except for the helper functions. However, since each helper is O(n) in worst case, the overall time complexity is O(n).

class Solution {
    public boolean validPalindrome(String s) {
        int left = 0;
        int right = s.length() - 1;
        
        while (left < right) {
            if (s.charAt(left) == s.charAt(right)) {
                left++;
                right--;
            } else {
                return isPalindrome(s, left + 1, right) || isPalindrome(s, left, right - 1);
            }
        }
        
        return true;
    }
    
    private boolean isPalindrome(String s, int left, int right) {
        while (left < right) {
            if (s.charAt(left) != s.charAt(right)) {
                return false;
            }
            left++;
            right--;
        }
        return true;
    }
}

Now, considering time constraints. For a string of length 1e5, the code would run in O(n) time. Each character is checked once, and in the worst case, the helper function runs O(n) steps once. So overall O(n) time.

Weakness is that in some cases, it could do two O(n) checks, but that's acceptable for the problem constraints.

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public boolean validPalindrome(String s) {
        int left = 0;
        int right = s.length() - 1;
        
        while (left < right) {
            if (s.charAt(left) == s.charAt(right)) {
                left++;
                right--;
            } else {
                return isPalindrome(s, left + 1, right) || isPalindrome(s, left, right - 1);
            }
        }
        
        return true;
    }
    
    private boolean isPalindrome(String s, int left, int right) {
        while (left < right) {
            if (s.charAt(left) != s.charAt(right)) {
                return false;
            }
            left++;
            right--;
        }
        return true;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Uses two pointers to check palindrome with at most one deletion. When a mismatch occurs, checks both possibilities (deleting left or right character) via helper function. Key strength is linear time and constant space. Weakness is potential for two O(n) checks in worst-case scenarios.
